text:
CT CHEST / ABDOMEN / PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 63-year-old female with metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma, sarcomatoid features, status post cytoreductive right radical nephrectomy and IVC thrombectomy. Currently on nivolumab 480 mg IV every 4 weeks (maintenance, following completion of ipilimumab/nivolumab induction x4 cycles). Recent SBRT to right lower lobe pulmonary metastasis (54 Gy in 3 fractions, completed approximately 6 weeks prior to this scan). Presenting for third restaging evaluation on systemic therapy. Prior imaging baseline established post-nephrectomy with bilateral pulmonary nodules as the predominant metastatic burden. First restaging scan demonstrated partial response; second restaging scan showed mixed response with right lower lobe lesion enlargement and a new right adrenal nodule (biopsy-confirmed adrenal metastasis). Now returning for response assessment.

COMPARISON: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast (second restaging scan, approximately 3 months prior); CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast (post-nephrectomy baseline); dedicated SBRT planning CT of the chest.

TECHNIQUE:
CT: Multi-detector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following administration of 100 mL of iohexol 350 mg/mL intravenous contrast, with acquisition in the portal venous phase through the abdomen and pelvis and arterial/venous phases through the chest. Slice thickness 2.5 mm reconstructed at 1.25 mm with multiplanar reformats in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes. Standard lung and soft tissue windows reviewed.

MRI Brain: Multiplanar, multisequence MRI of the brain was performed without and with 0.1 mmol/kg gadobutrol intravenous contrast. Sequences included: axial T1, axial T2, axial FLAIR, axial DWI/ADC, axial GRE/SWI, and post-contrast axial and coronal T1-weighted imaging with fat saturation. Slice thickness 5 mm for conventional sequences; 1 mm isotropic 3D MPRAGE post-contrast obtained.

---

FINDINGS:

MRI BRAIN:

Brain parenchyma: No intraparenchymal mass lesion or abnormal enhancing focus identified. No cortical or subcortical hemorrhage. No restricted diffusion to suggest acute ischemia. The ventricles are normal in size and configuration. The midline structures are intact. The grey-white matter differentiation is preserved.

Leptomeninges: No leptomeningeal or pachymeningeal nodular enhancement identified. Post-contrast images reviewed carefully at high resolution without suspicious nodular or linear meningeal enhancement.

Cerebrovascular: No acute territorial infarct. Mild periventricular and subcortical white matter T2/FLAIR hyperintensities are again noted, stable in extent and distribution, most consistent with chronic small vessel disease in the context of this patient's known hypertension. No new white matter lesions. No cavernous malformations.

Posterior fossa: The brainstem and cerebellum are unremarkable. No enhancing lesions in the posterior fossa. The fourth ventricle is patent.

Calvarium and skull base: No osseous destructive lesion involving the calvarium or skull base. Paranasal sinuses are clear.

Impression (Brain): No intracranial metastatic disease identified. No leptomeningeal carcinomatosis. Chronic small vessel ischemic white matter changes, stable.

---

CT CHEST:

Lungs and airways: The trachea and central airways are patent. No endobronchial lesion.

Right lower lobe - previously irradiated site (SBRT, 54 Gy/3 fx): The right lower lobe lesion targeted with stereotactic body radiotherapy has decreased in overall soft tissue conspicuity compared to the pre-SBRT scan. The lesion currently measures approximately 1.8 cm in greatest axial dimension (previously 2.4 cm at the time of SBRT planning). There is a surrounding zone of ground-glass opacity and mild consolidative change extending along adjacent lung parenchyma, consistent with expected post-radiation inflammatory change and fibrotic remodeling. The central soft tissue component is less distinct and more heterogeneous compared to its pre-treatment morphology, without a discrete well-defined nodular mass. The overall appearance is in keeping with a radiation treatment response; however, residual viable disease cannot be excluded on CT alone at this time interval.

Right upper lobe: A well-defined solid pulmonary nodule in the right upper lobe measures 1.8 cm in greatest axial dimension (previously 0.6 cm at second restaging; previously 1.2 cm at post-nephrectomy baseline). This represents substantial interval growth and is markedly larger than at any prior time point. The nodule has grown from a point of documented nadir to its current size, now exceeding even its pre-treatment size. No pleural tethering. No cavitation.

Left lower lobe: A pleural-based solid nodule in the posterobasal left lower lobe has grown to 1.4 cm (previously 0.5 cm at second restaging and 0.9 cm at baseline). The lesion now has a more lobulated, irregular contour compared to its previously smooth appearance and has more than doubled in size since the prior scan. A small satellite ground-glass opacity measuring approximately 5 mm is noted immediately adjacent to this lesion, which is new and raises concern for aerogenous spread or a second small discrete metastatic deposit.

Left upper lobe: A subpleural solid nodule in the left upper lobe measures 0.9 cm (previously 0.4 cm at second restaging; previously 0.7 cm at baseline). This nodule has more than doubled since the prior scan and is now larger than the original baseline measurement.

Additional pulmonary findings: No new discrete pulmonary nodules identified beyond those enumerated above. No pleural effusion. No pneumothorax. No pericardial effusion. Mild bibasilar atelectasis. No lymphangitic carcinomatosis pattern.

Mediastinum and hila: No significant mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy. The superior vena cava is patent. The heart is normal in size.

Chest wall and soft tissues: No chest wall invasion. Post-SBRT skin and subcutaneous changes not identified.

---

CT ABDOMEN AND PELVIS:

Liver: The liver is normal in size with smooth contours. A new hypodense lesion measuring approximately 1.6 cm is identified in segment VI of the liver (posteroinferior right lobe). On portal venous phase imaging, this lesion demonstrates peripheral rim enhancement with a relatively hypodense central component, a pattern atypical for a benign hepatic cyst or hemangioma. This lesion was not present on the prior restaging scan or the post-nephrectomy baseline CT. Given the patient's known metastatic history and the morphologic enhancement characteristics, this new lesion is highly suspicious for a hepatic metastasis. No additional hepatic lesions are identified. No biliary dilation. The hepatic veins and portal vein are patent.

Spleen: Normal in size and attenuation. No splenic lesions.

Pancreas: Unremarkable without focal mass or ductal dilation.

Adrenal glands: Left adrenal gland is normal. Right adrenal gland: The previously biopsied right adrenal nodule is again identified, now measuring 1.5 cm in greatest axial dimension (previously 1.3 cm at second restaging, previously not identified at baseline). The nodule has minimally enlarged since the last examination. Its morphology remains as a well-defined, slightly heterogeneous, hypoenhancing nodule, consistent with the known biopsy-confirmed adrenal metastasis.

Kidneys: Status post right radical nephrectomy; the right renal fossa demonstrates expected post-surgical changes with mild soft tissue stranding and fibrofatty infiltration but no discrete residual or recurrent soft tissue mass. The infrarenal IVC is patent without evidence of recurrent thrombus. The left kidney is present and normal in size and morphology. No hydronephrosis. No left renal mass.

Retroperitoneum: The right para-aortic and aortocaval nodal stations, which were involved with metastatic disease at initial staging and surgically dissected at the time of nephrectomy, show expected post-operative soft tissue changes. No discrete enlarged retroperitoneal lymph nodes measuring above the threshold for pathologic significance. The aorta is mildly ectatic in the infrarenal segment without aneurysmal change.

Bowel: The small and large bowel are unremarkable without obstruction, wall thickening, or mass lesion. No mesenteric adenopathy.

Pelvis: The urinary bladder is partially distended and unremarkable. No pelvic adenopathy. No pelvic free fluid.

Bones: No destructive osseous lesion involving the visualized thoracic, lumbar, or pelvic bones. No aggressive periosteal reaction. The right hip joint demonstrates mild degenerative changes with marginal osteophytosis and mild joint space narrowing, consistent with osteoarthritis.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. SYSTEMIC PROGRESSION OF METASTATIC CLEAR CELL RENAL CELL CARCINOMA on nivolumab maintenance therapy. Multiple sites of disease have grown compared to the prior scan, with overall disease burden substantially increased compared to both the second restaging scan and the original post-nephrectomy baseline.

2. RIGHT LOWER LOBE PULMONARY LESION: Following SBRT, the previously enlarging right lower lobe pulmonary metastasis has decreased to 1.8 cm with surrounding post-radiation changes; the overall appearance is consistent with a radiation treatment response and local disease control at this targeted site. Residual viable tumor cannot be definitively excluded.

3. RIGHT UPPER LOBE PULMONARY NODULE: Marked interval growth to 1.8 cm from 0.6 cm at the prior scan - this lesion has tripled in size, representing the most striking systemic progression outside the previously irradiated site.

4. LEFT LOWER LOBE PULMONARY NODULE: Interval growth to 1.4 cm from 0.5 cm, with new adjacent small satellite opacity, suggesting active disease.

5. LEFT UPPER LOBE PULMONARY NODULE: Interval growth to 0.9 cm from 0.4 cm.

6. NEW HEPATIC LESION: A new 1.6 cm peripherally enhancing hypodense lesion in hepatic segment VI, not present on prior imaging, highly suspicious for hepatic metastasis representing a new site of disease.

7. RIGHT ADRENAL METASTASIS: Known biopsy-confirmed right adrenal metastasis has minimally increased to 1.5 cm (from 1.3 cm), consistent with slow but ongoing progression.

8. NO INTRACRANIAL METASTASES on MRI brain; no leptomeningeal disease.

9. No osseous metastatic disease in the surveyed skeleton. Mild right hip degenerative changes.

10. Post-surgical right renal fossa changes remain stable. No recurrent IVC thrombus.

Radiologist: [Attending Radiologist, Abdominal Imaging and Oncologic Imaging Division]
Study reviewed with: Genitourinary Oncology Service
Report status: Final
